detail: Aaron Swartz / 'The Internet's Own Boy'. Feature ~30min (mid, tdm 31). R3 + SENSITIVE (death by suicide). Safe-handling lock; 35-years = theoretical maximum; no single-cause narrative.

14 sources (superseding indictment, DOJ/US Attorney release, MIT Abelson Report, JSTOR statement, family statement, Lessig, Orin Kerr, RSS 1.0 spec, Markdown, Creative Commons, Aaron's Law/congress.gov, The Internet's Own Boy; hashes pending). 18 claims with R3 + safe-handling wording locks. Facts web-verified at authoring.

~4,889 [VO:] words, 49 spans, 7 chapters (hook/opening/act1-4/ending+CTA). FK ~8.0, 0 em-dashes, 0 banned filler, And/But/Because/So starts <12%. Death stated once with locked wording; 988 lower-third; quiet CTA.
script_qc=pass; annotated qc_status=pass. Safe-handling, single-cause, 35-years-qualification, and wording locks all verified. validate_episode.py 23 -> PASS.
APR-0001 (script content) requested -> decision PENDING (owner gate). R3 content sign-off does not waive the required pre-publish dedicated legal/rights + safe-handling review.

state -> script_verified. Codex handoff design docs authored (_planning/codex_prompt_swartz.md + codex_local_swartz_build.md) and per-scene image prompts (04_scenes/codex_image_prompts.v001.md). Right-process build pending.
